summary: analysis-chart postinstall downloads and executes XOR-encrypted Windows shellcode
details: The package's postinstall hook (install-hook.js) downloads an opaque binary from https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v6/payload.bin — an unrelated personal GitHub account, not the package's claimed publisher — XORs the bytes with key 0x42, then uses koffi to call kernel32 VirtualAlloc / RtlMoveMemory / VirtualProtect(PAGE_EXECUTE_READ) / CreateThread to execute the decrypted shellcode in the npm install process on Windows. After execution, the hook writes a cleanup.js and spawns a detached node process that deletes the package directory, install-hook.js, cleanup.js, and removes 'analysis-chart' from the consumer's package.json — anti-forensics to hide that the compromise occurred. The advertised purpose ('lightweight chart analysis library') is a cover story; the shipped index.js statistics functions are unrelated to the dropper. Any developer running `npm install analysis-chart` on Windows executes attacker-controlled native code in their build environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
